title: CLSK Stock Slides As Q2 Earnings Miss Expectations
text: CleanSpark Inc. (NASDAQ: CLSK) declined 12.76% following disappointing second-quarter earnings. Revenue came in at $136.4M, missing the consensus estimate of $145.4M. The company reported a loss of $1.52 per share versus the expected loss of $0.56 per share. Profit margins remain deeply negative, with EBIT margin around -46.7% and overall profit margin near -53%. The article notes that operating income and net income remain sharply negative, and free cash flow for the recent period was about -$198.1M. This indicates the company is burning substantial cash while attempting to expand operations. Despite the poor earnings, CleanSpark maintains approximately $458.1M in cash reserves against total debt near $1.79B, providing some financial runway. The company also shows strong gross margin at 58.6%. The stock opened near $13.16, peaked at $14.34, then closed around $12.48, exhibiting the volatile intraday swings typical of high-beta securities in the Bitcoin mining sector.
